humanSummary.text: Initial audits found a P1 home/env path redaction gap plus P2/P3 serialization and profile-init coverage follow-ups. The P1 was repaired and re-audited clean; remaining findings are non-blocking DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Public stable report serialization can bypass report redaction
findings.description: CLI report writers use redacting wrappers, but public stable_json APIs for profile and inventory structs still serialize normalized raw structs directly. Library callers can accidentally use those APIs for report output and bypass the centralized redaction helper.

evidence.detail: Architecture audit observed GameProfile::stable_json and AssetInventoryManifest::stable_json serializing raw normalized structs while CLI report code uses private redacting wrappers.

findings.impact: Future report writers or library consumers could leak paths, keys, helper dumps, or private text despite KAIFUU-043 hardening CLI report paths.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: GameProfile and AssetInventoryManifest library serialization cannot leak sensitive report fields through report-intended APIs.
Raw deterministic serialization remains clearly separated from report-safe serialization if both are needed.
Tests cover library-call serialization and CLI report serialization for sensitive fields.

acceptanceCriteria: GameProfile and AssetInventoryManifest report-intended APIs apply the centralized redaction policy or expose an explicit raw/report-safe split.
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node KAIFUU-132 records this non-blocking audit finding.

findings.title: profile init redaction path lacks focused regression coverage
findings.description: The added CLI redaction tests cover generated profile report paths, but profile init has a distinct validate-then-write flow and should get focused regression coverage.

evidence.detail: Architecture audit noted profile init writes through a separate flow from the generated profile report test path.

findings.impact: A future profile init refactor could bypass redaction while generated profile report tests continue passing.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: profile init has a regression test with sensitive adapter payloads.
The test proves profile init output redacts paths, key material, helper dumps, and private text markers.

acceptanceCriteria: profile init output redaction is covered by a command-specific regression test.
auditFocus: profile init path bypassing generated report redaction tests
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node KAIFUU-133 records this non-blocking audit finding.
